title: ABNF, Tokens, Whitespace, Line Endings, Limits, and Parser Architecture · Go Semantics Case: Parse Validate Effect Phases
summary: A parser splits strings freely, accepts bare line feeds and whitespace before fields, has no byte limits, and mixes syntax recognition with application side effects. Planned artifact: Add and verify the parse validate effect phases increment in strict bounded incremental parser architecture. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate byte recognition, structural validation, semantic validation, policy, and effects so failure cannot partially authorize work.
Bound start line, each field line, total fields, field count, content, trailers, states, allocations, and parse time before consuming them.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Parse Validate Effect Phases · Theory Parse Validate Effect Phases
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 4823 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate strict grammar and resource limits before any application effect; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
